snp5.4:1.1: To the one unperturbed, who has seen the roots,
snp5.4:1.3: I have come with a question.
snp5.4:1.4: Because of what have many human seers
snp5.4:1.6: offered sacrifices to devas here in the world?
snp5.4:1.7:
I ask you, Blessed One.
Please tell me.
The Buddha:
snp5.4:2.4: who have offered sacrifices to devas
snp5.4:2.5: here in the world, Puṇṇaka, hoping for more of this state of being,
snp5.4:2.6:
offered their sacrifices because of aging.
Puṇṇaka:
snp5.4:3.4: who have offered sacrifices to devas here in the world:
snp5.4:3.5: Have they, Blessed One, heeding the path of sacrifice, dear sir,
snp5.4:3.7:
I ask you, Blessed One.
Please tell me.
The Buddha:
snp5.4:4.1: They hoped for, liked, longed for, so they sacrificed—
snp5.4:4.3:
they longed for sensuality, dependent on gain.
I tell you:
snp5.4:4.4: Those who take on the yoke of sacrifice, impassioned with the passion for becoming,
snp5.4:4.5:
have not crossed over birth & aging.
Puṇṇaka:
snp5.4:5.1: If those who take on the yoke of sacrifice
snp5.4:5.4: then who in the world, dear sir, of beings divine & human
snp5.4:5.6:
I ask you, Blessed One.
Please tell me.
The Buddha:
snp5.4:6.1: He who has fathomed the high & low in the world,
snp5.4:6.3: for whom there is nothing perturbing in the world—
snp5.4:6.5: he, I tell you, has crossed over birth & aging.
